name: United Trust Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit with online and mobile banking services.
description_long:
United Trust Bank is a federally insured financial institution operating under FDIC protection, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank serves both retail and business customers through its main website at utbhome.com, providing traditional banking services to individuals and companies seeking deposit products and account management.

The bank's service offerings include personal checking accounts with multiple account options, personal savings products, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs). Customers can access their accounts through online banking platforms and a mobile app available on both Apple and Google platforms. The bank has partnered with Zelle® to enable customers to send and receive money directly from their accounts to enrolled recipients within minutes through online banking or mobile channels. Business customers have access to dedicated online banking tools separate from retail customers.

United Trust Bank distinguishes itself through FDIC insurance coverage, which provides protection for customer deposits. The bank maintains physical branch locations and offers customer service support via phone at (708) 728-9900. They provide financial calculators on their website to help customers with account planning, and maintain a community presence through events such as annual shred events in 2026. The bank observes standard federal holidays and maintains closure schedules for Memorial Day, Juneteenth, Independence Day, Labor Day, Columbus Day, Veteran's Day, Thanksgiving, and Christmas.

As a regional bank, United Trust Bank offers basic mainstream banking services without specialization in niche lending products, investment services, or premium wealth management. The website provides limited detail on specific interest rates, account fees, or minimum balance requirements, requiring customers to contact the bank directly or visit in person for comprehensive product information. The bank appears to serve a local or regional market rather than positioning itself as a national or digital-first institution. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts with multiple account varieties
Online banking platform for retail customers
Online banking platform for business customers
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Multiple personal checking account options to suit different customer needs
Zelle® integration for fast peer-to-peer money transfers within minutes
Dedicated online and mobile banking platforms for both retail and business customers
Separate business online banking portal for commercial customers
Physical branch locations with customer service phone support at (708) 728-9900
Multiple deposit products including savings, money market, and CDs with published rates
cons: Limited information on website regarding account fees, minimum balances, and rate details
No indication of nationwide presence or digital-first banking capabilities
Website does not display current interest rates for savings, money market, or CD products
No mention of personal loans, credit cards, mortgages, or investment products
Requires phone contact or in-person visit for most account and pricing information

contact.address: 12330 S Harlem Ave, Palos Heights, IL 60463

best_for: Customers seeking FDIC-insured deposit accounts in the bank's service region
Individuals and small business owners needing basic checking and savings products
People who prefer traditional banking with physical branch access and phone support
Customers interested in peer-to-peer money transfers via Zelle®
diagnosis: United Trust Bank is best for regional customers seeking straightforward FDIC-insured deposit products and basic checking/savings accounts with traditional branch banking. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal detail on rates, fees, and account terms, requiring direct contact with the bank for complete product information and pricing.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
